name: improve-codebase-architecture
description: Codebase-wide architecture health scan producing deepening candidates (proposal-only)

positive_triggers.input: I just inherited this 200k-LOC codebase; where should I start letting agents ramp?
positive_triggers.reason: On-boarding scan, codebase-wide perspective needed before agent ramp

positive_triggers.input: It's been a month since we last looked at the architecture; let's do a weekly review
Agents keep producing garbage; I think the codebase itself is part of the problem
positive_triggers.reason: Codebase quality hypothesis, needs full-repo lens

negative_triggers.input: Refactor this 500-line module, it's hard to read
negative_triggers.reason: Per-diff scope, route to OMO refactor / ponytail-review / remove-ai-slops

negative_triggers.input: Our API endpoint is returning 500s, debug it
Add a new feature X to the codebase
negative_triggers.reason: New feature design, route to brainstorming → spec-driven-development

behavioral_evals.scenario: User inherits 200k-LOC codebase and asks for on-boarding guidance
expected_behavior: Agent first inventories codebase (subsystems, boundaries, concept-sprawl hotspots)
Agent surfaces 3+ confusion candidates from Matt Pocock's three checks
Agent scores each candidate on Ousterhout 4-axis deep/shallow framework
Agent writes output to .omo/plans/<id>/architecture-review.md, NOT the OMO plan
Agent does NOT run Edit/Bash to apply any refactor — proposal-only
Agent hands off to user/team for prioritization, defers to incremental-implementation for action

notes: Behavioral evals are warning-level until promoted via upstream issue tracker. Proposal-only contract enforced by Process § 5 — agent must NOT execute changes inside this skill.
